issues.comment: The OR condition `retryCount: { gt: 1 }` deletes ALL workflow reminders with retryCount > 1 regardless of method, but this function is SMS-specific. Non-SMS reminders (email, etc.) will be incorrectly deleted when they fail multiple times.

The retryCount increment `reminder.retryCount + 1` is a read-modify-write operation that could lose updates if multiple instances process the same reminder concurrently, though this is partially mitigated by the `scheduled: false` filter.
The retryCount increment `reminder.retryCount + 1` in the catch block has the same race condition risk as the else block, where concurrent processing could lead to lost retry count updates.
